summary: Controls the SMB client DisableLargeMtu value under LanmanWorkstation. Large MTU usually improves throughput on modern networks, but some troubleshooting cases temporarily disable it when old network gear or filtering devices behave badly.

setting.name: SMB Client Large MTU
setting.casual_explanation: Large MTU lets SMB send larger requests over the network. On modern Windows networks this usually helps file-copy and share performance.
setting.why_people_change_it: People keep large MTU enabled for better SMB throughput. They only disable it when investigating compatibility problems with old devices, buggy middleboxes, or unusual network inspection setups.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Keeping large MTU enabled is usually better for performance. Disabling it can help with rare compatibility issues, but it can reduce throughput and SMB efficiency.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents DisableLargeMtu under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanWorkstation\Parameters. A value of 0 keeps large MTU enabled, while 1 disables it and limits request sizing behavior.

allowed_values.label: Windows managed default
allowed_values.meaning: Windows uses its built-in SMB client default, which Microsoft documents as large MTU enabled by default.

allowed_values.label: Large MTU enabled
allowed_values.meaning: Large MTU support stays enabled for SMB client connections.

allowed_values.label: Large MTU disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Large MTU is turned off and SMB uses smaller request sizing behavior.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableLargeMtu = 0, which matches the documented enabled state for SMB large MTU support.

windows_defaults.label: Windows SMB client default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Supported modern Windows SMB client releases

states.rationale: Microsoft documents large MTU as enabled by default, so an explicit override is not required for the normal baseline.

windows_defaults.notes: This record documents the effective Windows behavior rather than assuming the registry value is always explicitly present.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want Windows to keep normal SMB client behavior
avoid_for: People who need an explicit large-MTU override

states.rationale: Leave the explicit override absent and keep the Windows baseline.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want standard Windows SMB behavior without forcing an override.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are troubleshooting a network problem and need to force one side of the setting.
tradeoffs: Windows keeps its normal large-MTU behavior.
You do not document an explicit local override.

recommended_profiles.label: Modern throughput
intended_for: Users who want better SMB performance
avoid_for: Rare environments with old or fragile network devices

states.rationale: Explicitly keep large MTU enabled for SMB client connections.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the app to force the normal high-throughput SMB behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are chasing a compatibility issue with old networking equipment.
tradeoffs: This can expose rare compatibility issues that only appear with larger requests.

recommended_profiles.label: Compatibility troubleshooting
avoid_for: Users who just want the normal modern SMB baseline

states.rationale: Turn off large MTU for troubleshooting or legacy compatibility testing.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You have a specific reason to suspect SMB large-request handling is part of a problem.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are enabling it blindly without an actual compatibility symptom.
tradeoffs: Can help isolate rare network compatibility problems.
Can reduce SMB performance on normal modern networks.

evidence.title: Microsoft performance tuning for SMB file servers
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents DisableLargeMtu under LanmanWorkstation and states that large MTU is enabled by default, with 1 used to limit request size.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableLargeMtu = 0 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the exact registry value and meaning used by the app, including the default enabled behavior for large MTU, and the app writes that documented state with a clean rollback story.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanWorkstation\Parameters\DisableLargeMtu. Large MTU is enabled by default. You can prevent connections to file shares that don't support large MTU by setting this parameter to 1.
validation_proof.notes: The Microsoft SMB performance tuning page documents the exact client registry value under LanmanWorkstation and states that large MTU is enabled by default. The app writes 0 to keep the documented enabled state.
